behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_CANOPYTP::look' (examine) handler for the CANOPYTP object. When the player examines the object, the script displays a single short description/bark reading "canopy ".
After emitting the bark the process suspends/ends; there are no conditionals, menus, flag reads/writes, or external calls in this snippet.
Behavior is minimal and purely presentational — it provides the in-world short description for the canopy trigger.
notable_control_flow: Entry into U8P_CANOPYTP::look triggers an immediate Bark node with the text "canopy ".
Following the Bark the process executes a suspend/end node. There is no branching or additional logic.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "canopy " intentional (formatting artifact) or meaningful in the game UI?
Is U8P_CANOPYTP::look ever extended by other scripts or runtime hooks (none referenced here)?

main_function.name: U8P_CANOPYTP::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short examine/description text for the CANOPYTP object when the player uses the U8P_CANOPYTP::look/examine action.
entry_conditions: Invoked when the player performs the 'U8P_CANOPYTP::look' (examine) action on the CANOPYTP object (inferred from process classification 0x0208 and function name).
